channel.title: Machine Learning on Frank Huang
channel.description: Recent content in Machine Learning on Frank Huang

item.title: Structuring machine learning project week 1 --
item.description: Orthogonalization fit training set well on cost: fit dev set well on cost: fit test set well on cost: perform well on real world: evaluation metric precision: of the examples that your classifier recognizes as cats recall: of all the images that really are cats, what percentage were correctly recognized by your classifier F1 score: combine precision and recall, average: harmonic mean distribution of dev and test set size on dev/test set?

item.title: How to improve your neural net week 2: note of deeplearning.ai from coursera
item.description: This week’s topic is about speeding up the learning process. Mini batch epoch: a single run through the whole training examples. If we are using mini batch technique with T batches, we will run T times of gradient descent wheras without mini batch, we will get only 1 time of gradient descent. Notation $$ a^{[l]\{t\}(m)} $$ means the the activation value of m examples in t minibatch in lth layer.

item.title: How to improve your neural net week 1: note of deeplearning.ai from coursera
item.description: How to split data into train/dev/test The goal of having dev set is to select the model having the best performance for the final test. Small data situation: 60%/20%/20% or so Large data situation: 98%/1%/1% or so Why does the dev and test set should be better to have a same distribution? The goal of having dev set is to select a model with the best performance which will be tested in the test set.

item.title: Multilayer Neural network -- note of deeplearning.ai from coursera
item.description: Lost function for logistic classification: $$ J=-\frac{1}{m} \sum_{i=0}^{m}\left(y^{(i)} \log \left(a^{[2](i)}\right)+\left(1-y^{(i)}\right) \log \left(1-a^{[2](i)}\right)\right) $$ Several questions to take away: forward phase: 1. what to cache in layer l? backprop phase: 1. what derivative value should be calculated for layer l? 2. what will be the output for layer l? 3. what will the input for layer l? 4. why is the update parameter work by subtracting the derivative? that is $$ \boldsymbol{W}^{[I]}=\boldsymbol{W}^{[I]}-\alpha d \boldsymbol{W}^{[I]} $$ 5.

item.title: My thougts about Linear Regression
item.description: Suppose we have a for variables that are correlated lasso regression ridge regression spark-submit kept showing: yarn.Client: Application report for application (state: ACCEPTED) spark 参数配置 –conf -> shell parameter -> conf/spark-defaults.conf num-executors executor-cores spark.default.parallelism total-executor-cores memory setting: memory is splitted into 3 parts: for tasks(20%), for shuffle(20%), for storage(60%) spark.storage.memoryFraction: cache/persist operation spark.shuffle.memoryFraction: memory for shuffle manipulation executor-memory: total memory for each executor driver-memory 参数说明：Total cores for all executors.

item.title: Support vector machine
item.description: v is the point to be classified. w is a vector points to the hyperplane perpendicularly. $$hyperplane:y=w\cdot \xrightarrow { V } +b$$ There are 2 classes which are black and red respectively. if w v + b > 0, then the v belongs to black if w v + b < 0, then the v belongs to red if w * v + v = 0, then the v is on the decision boundary which is also the hyperplane.
